reference_impl: official Boltz-2 (torch + pytorch-lightning, GPU -- vast.ai RTX 3090)
reference_commit: boltz 2.2.1 (pip-installed on the vast.ai instance; upstream jwohlzend/boltz)
command: boltz predict examples/ubiquitin_no_msa.yaml --out_dir <out> --seed <N> --recycling_steps 3 --sampling_steps 200 --diffusion_samples 1 --accelerator gpu --no_kernels --override --preprocessing-threads 4  && python3 scripts/boltz2_ref_layout.py <out>/boltz_results_ubiquitin_no_msa <harness_dir>

settings.dtype: bf16-mixed (pytorch-lightning AMP, same precision recipe as the CPU reference; --no_kernels forces the torch-einsum triangle path = the SAME kernel the qb1 CPU reference uses, only the execution device differs)
settings.execution_device: NVIDIA RTX 3090 24GB (vast.ai on-demand instance, ssh9.vast.ai:16304, label pharma-gpu-ref-migration-rtx3090)

provenance: Generated 2026-07-21 on a rented vast.ai RTX 3090 (on-demand) with the pinned official boltz 2.2.1, --no_kernels so the torch-einsum triangle kernel matches the committed CPU reference (only the execution device differs: GPU vs CPU), same seeds/settings as the CPU fixture (nomsa_200step_1sample_3recycle_bf16). Per-seed wall ~1 min on the 3090. GPU self-floor R (CA-RMSD 1.510 A, 1-lDDT 0.069) recorded in docs/pharma-benchmark-data/boltz2-ubiquitin-gpu-ref-floor.json. This is the GPU-reference companion to the committed CPU reference (task tt-bio-pharma-benchmark-gpu-reference-migration).
invalidation_rule: Regenerate this fixture ONLY when the pinned reference_commit/version changes or the settings above change. For any other change (device seeds, device code, release tag) the fixture is reused as-is and only the device side re-runs.
